title: Search Trees: Insertion (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the student tracks the parent incorrectly, base the attachment marks on whether they attach the node consistently using their own parent variable.
This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is insert(Node root, Node insert); the Python function is insert(root, insert). Both are correct.
The node class has fields: key (int), left (Node), right (Node), and parent (Node). Submissions may use any variable names for the traversal pointers.
Any correct iterative or recursive BST insertion that preserves the BST property is acceptable.

rubric_points.name: Traversal to the insertion point
rubric_points.target: A loop (or recursive call) walks from the root to a null position, moving left when insert.key < x.key and right otherwise, until the correct empty slot is reached.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No traversal is attempted; the node is attached directly to the root or the method returns without navigating the tree.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A loop or recursive call is present but the direction logic is absent or incorrect (e.g., always moves left, or uses a condition unrelated to key comparison), so the correct null insertion point is not reliably reached.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: A loop (or recursion) correctly walks from the root to a null child position, moving left when insert.key < x.key and right otherwise, stopping when the null slot is found.

rubric_points.name: Parent tracking during traversal
rubric_points.target: A separate pointer (e.g., y) is maintained and updated at each step of the traversal, so that after the loop it holds the parent of the null insertion point.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No parent pointer is tracked during traversal; after the loop there is no reference to the node that should become the parent of insert.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A parent variable is declared but is not correctly updated at every step of the traversal (e.g., updated only for one branch, or assigned after the loop rather than inside it), so it does not reliably hold the correct parent after traversal.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: A parent pointer is initialised before the loop and updated to the current node at every step, so after the loop it correctly holds the last non-null node visited (the parent of the insertion point).

rubric_points.name: Attaching insert as left or right child
rubric_points.target: After traversal, insert is attached as the left child of the parent when insert.key < parent.key, and as the right child otherwise.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: insert is not attached to the parent node at all, or is attached without comparing insert.key with the parent's key (e.g., always set as left child).

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: insert is attached to the parent node, but the left/right direction is incorrect (e.g., comparison direction is reversed, or the same condition from traversal is reused without adapting to the parent).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: insert is attached as parent.left when insert.key < parent.key, and as parent.right otherwise, correctly placing it in the BST.

rubric_points.name: Setting insert.parent
rubric_points.target: insert.parent is set to the parent node found during traversal (y), establishing the upward link.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: insert.parent is not set, or is assigned a value unrelated to the traversal (e.g., always null, or set to the traversal pointer x which is null after the loop).

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: insert.parent is set but using the wrong variable (e.g., the live traversal pointer instead of the tracked parent pointer), giving an incorrect or null parent reference.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: insert.parent is correctly assigned to the parent pointer (y) tracked during traversal, giving insert the correct upward link in the tree.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: The method returns a valid BST after insertion for all inputs, including inserting into an empty tree.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Method returns null or leaves the tree unmodified for most inputs.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Insertion works for simple cases (e.g., inserting as a direct child of root) but fails for deeper insertions, or the empty tree case (y == null) is not handled, or the return value is wrong (e.g., always returns insert instead of root for non-empty trees).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: The BST property is preserved after insertion for all valid inputs. The empty tree case is handled by returning insert as the new root, and the original root is returned for non-empty trees.

buckets.label: Internally consistent but incorrect final result
buckets.description: Logic follows consistently from the student's own traversal and attachment approach, but the resulting tree is incorrect due to a systematic error (e.g., consistently wrong direction or parent pointer).
